section.heading: Definitions

paragraph.content: the term “at-risk” has the meaning given that term in section 6472 of title 20;

subparagraph.content: a unit of local government that is in compliance with the requirements of part B of subchapter II; or
a nonprofit organization in partnership with a unit of local government described in subparagraph (A);

paragraph.chapeau: the term “delinquency prevention program” means a delinquency prevention program that is evidence-based or promising and that may include—

subparagraph.content: alcohol and substance abuse prevention or treatment services;
tutoring and remedial education, especially in reading and mathematics;
child and adolescent health and mental health services;
the teaching that individuals are and should be held accountable for their actions;
assistance in the development of job training skills;

subparagraph.chapeau: coordination of a continuum of services that may include—

clause.content: emergency, transitional and permanent housing assistance;
alternative school resources for youth who have dropped out of school or demonstrate chronic truancy;
targeted gang prevention, intervention and exit services;
training and education programs for pregnant teens and teen parents; and
pre-release, post-release, and reentry services to assist detained and incarcerated youth with transitioning back into and reentering the community; and

subparagraph.content: other data-driven evidence-based or promising prevention programs;

paragraph.chapeau: the term “local policy board”, when used with respect to an eligible entity, means a policy board that the eligible entity will engage in the development of the eligible entity’s plan described in section 11313(e)(5) of this title, and that includes—

subparagraph.content: not fewer than 15 and not more than 21 members; and 11 So in original. The word “and” probably should not appear.

clause.content: public agencies and private nonprofit organizations serving juveniles and their families; and

subparagraph.content: at least one representative of the faith community, one adjudicated youth, and one parent of an adjudicated youth; and
in the case of an eligible entity described in paragraph (1)(B), a representative of the nonprofit organization of the eligible entity;

paragraph.content: the term “mentoring” means matching 1 adult with 1 or more youths for the purpose of providing guidance, support, and encouragement through regularly scheduled meetings for not less than 9 months;
the term “State advisory group” means the advisory group appointed by the chief executive officer of a State under a plan described in section 11133(a) of this title; and
the term “State entity” means the State agency designated under section 11133(a)(1) of this title or the entity receiving funds under section 11133(d) of this title.

section.sourceCredit: (Pub. L. 93–415, title V, § 502, as added Pub. L. 107–273, div. C, title II, § 12222(a), Nov. 2, 2002, 116 Stat. 1894; amended Pub. L. 115–385, title III, § 302, Dec. 21, 2018, 132 Stat. 5145.)

note.heading: Codification
note.p: Section was formerly classified to section 5781 of Title 42, The Public Health and Welfare, prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section.

note.heading: Prior Provisions
note.p: A prior section 502 of title V of Pub. L. 93–415, as added Pub. L. 102–586, § 5(a), Nov. 4, 1992, 106 Stat. 5027, related to findings, prior to the general amendment of title V of Pub. L. 93–415 by Pub. L. 107–273.

Another prior section 502 of Pub. L. 93–415, title V, Sept. 7, 1974, 88 Stat. 1134, amended section 5032 of Title 18, Crimes and Criminal Procedure, prior to repeal by Pub. L. 115–385, title III, § 307, Dec. 21, 2018, 132 Stat. 5152.

note.heading: Amendments
note.p: 2018—Pub. L. 115–385 amended section generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “In this subchapter, the term ‘State advisory group’ means the advisory group appointed by the chief executive officer of a State under a plan described in section 11133(a) of this title.”

note.heading: Effective Date of 2018 Amendment
note.p: Amendment by Pub. L. 115–385 not applicable with respect to funds appropriated for any fiscal year that begins before Dec. 21, 2018, see section 3 of Pub. L. 115–385, set out as a note under section 11102 of this title.

note.heading: Effective Date
note.p: Pub. L. 107–273, div. C, title II, § 12222(b), Nov. 2, 2002, 116 Stat. 1896, as amended by Pub. L. 108–7, div. B, title I, § 110(1), Feb. 20, 2003, 117 Stat. 67, provided that: “The amendment made by subsection (a) [see Tables for classification] shall take effect on the effective date provided in section 12102(b) [set out as a note under section 10401 of this title], and shall not apply with respect to grants made before such date.”

note.heading: Short Title
note.p: For short title of title V of Pub. L. 93–415, which is classified to this subchapter, as the “Incentive Youth Promise Grants for Local Delinquency Prevention Programs Act of 2018”, see section 501 of Pub. L. 93–415, set out as a Short Title of 1974 Act note under section 10101 of this title.

note.heading: GAO Studies and Reports
note.p: Pub. L. 102–586, § 5(b), Nov. 4, 1992, 106 Stat. 5029, as amended by Pub. L. 104–316, title I, § 122(n), Oct. 19, 1996, 110 Stat. 3838; Pub. L. 108–271, § 8(b), July 7, 2004, 118 Stat. 814, provided that: “Under such conditions as the Comptroller General of the United States determines appropriate, the Government Accountability Office may conduct studies and report to Congress on the effects of the program established by subsection (a) [enacting former title V of Pub. L. 93–415, former 42 U.S.C. 5781–5785] in encouraging States and units of general local government to comply with the requirements of part B of title II of the Juvenile Justice and Delinquency Prevention Act of 1974 (42 U.S.C. 5631–5633) [now 34 U.S.C. 11131–11133].”
